channel.title: ainews — Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: Grok Imagine Quality Mode API
item.description: xAI exposed Grok Imagine Quality Mode through the API, giving developers programmatic access to the higher-fidelity image generation tier previously limited to the Grok app. Pricing is per-image at the higher quality cost band.

item.title: Connectors now on Grok Web
item.description: xAI rolled connectors out to Grok on the web, letting users link Gmail, Google Drive, GitHub, and other services so Grok can search and act across them. Mirrors agent-style integrations already shipped by ChatGPT and Claude.

item.title: Scale AI launches SWE Atlas Refactoring Leaderboard — Claude Opus 4.7 leads
item.description: Scale AI published a new SWE Atlas Refactoring leaderboard scoring how well coding agents perform multi-file refactors on real repos. Claude Code with Opus 4.7 currently tops the board, followed by Codex and GPT-5 variants.

item.title: Real-Time Performance Monitoring and Faster Debugging with NCCL Inspector and Prometheus
item.description: NVIDIA describes NCCL Inspector, a Prometheus-compatible tool for live-monitoring NVIDIA Collective Communications Library traffic across multi-GPU training jobs. It surfaces stragglers and failed all-reduces faster than log-scraping, useful for teams running large distributed training.

item.title: DeepMind: AlphaEvolve Gemini-powered coding agent accelerating quantum, biotech, logistics
item.description: Google DeepMind highlighted AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent that iteratively proposes and tests algorithm changes. They credit it with measurable speedups in quantum simulation, protein workflows, and logistics scheduling.

item.title: Parloa builds service agents customers want to talk to
item.description: OpenAI case study on Parloa using its Realtime API to run multilingual phone-and-chat customer-service agents at scale for enterprises. Parloa reports faster handoffs and higher containment when GPT-class voice models replace legacy IVR.

item.title: Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API
item.description: OpenAI shipped GPT-Realtime-2 with GPT-5-class reasoning for low-latency voice agents, plus a faster Whisper variant and a new Realtime-Translate model covering 70 source languages and 13 outputs. The release targets production voice agents in the API and presages a ChatGPT Voice upgrade.

item.title: Commvault warns ANZ organisations are scaling AI faster than they can secure it
item.description: A Commvault report says Australian and New Zealand organisations are deploying AI faster than they can build resilience or governance for it, with model and data backup, lineage, and rollback emerging as gaps. Practitioners are urged to extend disaster recovery plans to AI assets.

item.title: NCSC and Five Eyes cyber agencies warn channel partners over agentic AI risks
item.description: New Zealand's NCSC joined Five Eyes counterparts in a joint advisory urging IT channel partners to harden controls before deploying agentic AI for clients. The guidance flags identity, action-scope, and audit-trail gaps as the highest-risk areas.

item.title: AI factory race reaches New Zealand: Datagrid plans build near Invercargill
item.description: Singapore-based Datagrid is planning what would be New Zealand's first dedicated AI training data centre near Invercargill, leveraging Southland power. Coverage flags broad questions over grid impact, water use, and economic returns.

item.title: Government invests in Aimer Farming AI for pasture decisions
item.description: MPI is co-funding Aimer Farming's AI platform that recommends rotational grazing decisions from pasture imagery and weather data. The deal is one of the few NZ government commitments to applied AI in primary production.

item.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
item.description: ServiceNow-AI on Hugging Face documents subtle numerical drift between vLLM V0 and V1 that silently broke RL training reward signals. The post argues teams should validate inference parity before tuning RL hyperparameters when migrating engines.

item.title: Accelerating Gemma 4: faster inference with multi-token prediction drafters
item.description: Google released Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) drafters for the Gemma 4 family, delivering up to 3x faster inference without quality loss. A lightweight drafter predicts multiple tokens ahead while the larger model verifies them in parallel using speculative decoding; available under Apache 2.0 with support for Hugging Face, vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama.

item.title: xAI: New Compute Partnership with Anthropic
item.description: xAI announced a compute partnership with Anthropic — the two frontier labs will share infrastructure, with xAI providing Anthropic with access to its training capacity. The deal is unusual given the labs' otherwise opposed positioning on AI safety, and signals how acute compute scarcity is for every frontier player.

item.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI Factories
item.description: NVIDIA detailed Spectrum-X Ethernet as the scale-out fabric for gigascale AI training clusters, with the new Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol developed with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft, and OpenAI. The pitch is that Ethernet, hardened with MRC and Spectrum-X switches, can match InfiniBand for AI factories that approach a million accelerators.

item.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
item.description: Hugging Face is hardening its Open ASR Leaderboard against benchmark gaming by introducing a private holdout split. Speech-recognition models will now be evaluated on data they cannot have trained on, reducing the value of overfitting to public test sets and giving more credible relative rankings.

item.title: OpenAI: How frontier enterprises are building an AI advantage
item.description: OpenAI published a B2B Signals research note on how leading enterprises deepen AI adoption. The piece argues frontier customers are scaling Codex-powered agentic workflows beyond pilot use and treating durable agent infrastructure — not one-shot prompts — as the source of competitive advantage.

item.title: Introducing ChatGPT Futures: Class of 2026
item.description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT 26, seeking 26 students and recent graduates already building with AI. The cohort — the first to go through college with ChatGPT — gets invited to OpenAI HQ in June 2026 for a summit; applications closed April 5.

item.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
item.description: NVIDIA and ServiceNow are partnering to deliver autonomous AI agents into enterprise environments, combining NVIDIA's AI infrastructure with ServiceNow's workflow platform. The collaboration targets agentic AI deployment at scale, moving beyond simple prompts to multi-step task execution inside enterprise systems.

item.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
item.description: Google announced a partnership with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition. The contest invites filmmakers to create works that imagine positive AI futures, with Google providing AI tooling for entrants.

item.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
item.description: NVIDIA's developer blog provides a technical walkthrough of building multimodal agentic AI for automotive cockpits, covering the full cloud-to-car architecture. The guide addresses how in-vehicle agents can reason, plan, and respond across voice, vision, and sensor inputs using NVIDIA's DriveOS stack.

item.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
item.description: NVIDIA argues that as AI systems shift from single-model inference to multi-agent coordination, hardware, software, and AI model design must be co-designed together — not built in isolation. The post outlines an 'extreme co-design' framework for scaling agentic system complexity without compounding failure modes.

item.title: Anthropic: Agents for financial services and insurance
item.description: Anthropic released 10 preconfigured Claude agent templates for banks, asset managers, and insurers: pitchbooks, earnings analysis, credit memos, underwriting, KYC, month-end close, and insurance claims. Integrates with Moody's, Dun & Bradstreet, and Microsoft 365; Claude scored 88% accuracy on insurance claims versus human experts.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Instant System Card
item.description: OpenAI published the system card for GPT-5.5 Instant, documenting capabilities, evaluations, and safety testing for the new variant. The card details performance benchmarks and the model's positioning relative to other GPT-5 variants.

item.title: GPT-5.5 Instant: smarter, clearer, and more personalized
item.description: OpenAI has replaced ChatGPT's default model with GPT-5.5 Instant, which delivers 52.5% fewer hallucinations than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts across medicine, law, and finance. The model gains personalization by referencing past conversations, files, and Gmail — initially for Plus and Pro users — and ships to all ChatGPT tiers.

item.title: NZ firms warned overseas businesses aren't getting a return on AI - MPs report
item.description: A NZ Parliamentary report cautions local businesses that overseas AI investments often fail to generate returns, while still encouraging domestic adoption. The report references international evidence showing many enterprise AI deployments underperform on ROI.

item.title: Massey University study: generative AI is replacing entry-level creative roles in NZ
item.description: A Massey University pilot study finds generative AI is displacing junior roles in New Zealand creative industries, with productivity gains often captured by overseas firms rather than local agencies.

item.title: Five Eyes guidance: don't give agentic AI the keys to sensitive data
item.description: Five Eyes intelligence agencies (US, UK, Canada, Australia, NZ) issued joint guidance urging organisations to limit agentic AI access to sensitive data. The guidance is directly relevant to NZ insurers and government agencies considering autonomous workflows.

item.title: OpenAI and PwC collaborate to reimagine the office of the CFO
item.description: OpenAI and PwC have announced a partnership to deploy AI agents across enterprise finance functions, targeting forecasting, reporting, and internal controls. The collaboration positions AI agents as the primary interface for CFO-level decision workflows, building on PwC's existing enterprise client base.

item.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
item.description: NVIDIA's cuOpt library has introduced 'Agent Skills' — modular AI capabilities that let agents autonomously solve supply chain optimization problems including vehicle routing, scheduling, and capacity allocation. The post demonstrates integrating cuOpt skills into agent pipelines for real-time decision support under volatile demand.
